##### Any pull request raised here should be submitted according to this template or it may be flagged with `Needs more information`. No action will be taken until the required context is provided.

# Pull request details

* **Please check if this PR fulfills these requirements**
- [ ] The commit message(s) explain the changes clearly
- [ ] Tests for the change have been added or updated when appropriate
- [ ] Documentation has been added or updated when appropriate
- [ ] Screenshots, logs, or recordings are included for UI/operator-surface changes

* **What kind of change does this PR introduce?**
- [ ] Bug fix
- [ ] Added feature
- [ ] Documentation update
- [ ] Build / packaging / release change
- [ ] Other - explain below

* **What is the current behavior?**

_Describe the current behavior, failure mode, or limitation. Link the issue if one exists._

* **What is the new behavior?**

_Describe what changes after this PR. For UI changes, include the affected surfaces such as `rotator`, `radio`, `aprs`, `settings`, or `lite`._

* **How was this tested?**

_List the exact checks run. Include browser/platform details for UI changes and any radio / APRS hardware context if applicable._

* **Does this PR introduce a breaking change?**

_Describe any user-visible, API, configuration, or operator-workflow breakage. If none, say `No`._

* **Operator / environment context**

_If relevant, include: browser, OS, rig model, transport mode (`usb` / `wifi`), fake-pass mode usage, APRS operating mode, and whether the issue was reproduced on real hardware._

* **Other information**

_Add anything else that will help review this PR quickly._

<hr>

###### DO NOT DELETE OR EDIT anything below this

<hr>

<sub><b>Note 1:</b> Make sure to add all the information needed to understand and validate the change. If essential information is missing, the PR may be marked <code>Needs more information</code>.</sub>

<sub><b>Note 2:</b> If the change affects operator-facing flows, include enough detail for a reviewer to reproduce the behavior on the relevant OrbitDeck surface.</sub>
